home.streakSheetSubtitle: A day counts when Norway Score reaches {{count}} or more.

streakRules.score: Finish enough daily protocol tasks to reach {{count}} Norway Score.
streakRules.calendar: The streak follows calendar days in your local timezone.
streakRules.reset: Miss the threshold for a day and the next completed day starts a new streak.

protocol.wholeGrain: Choose oats, rye or another whole grain
protocol.legumes: Build one meal around beans, peas or lentils
protocol.fishOrLegumes: Choose fish or legumes instead of processed meat
protocol.vegetableHalfPlate: Make vegetables the largest part of one meal
protocol.nutsSeeds: Add a small handful of unsalted nuts or seeds
protocol.processedSwap: Swap one low-quality choice for a more nutrient-dense food
protocol.mealPrep: Prep a Nordic whole-food meal for tomorrow

cold.title: Cold
cold.planSubtitle: Short shower stages. Open water is a separate safety decision.

guide.shower: This is a shower habit challenge, not a medical dose. Stop early whenever control slips.
guide.breathe: Breathe normally. No breath holds, hyperventilation or underwater practice.
guide.solo: Never enter open cold water alone. Use known entry and exit points.
guide.exit: Exit before panic, numbness or dizziness. Warm up calmly after.

session.coldSafetyHint: Keep breathing normal. Stop before numbness, dizziness or panic.
session.finishedSubtitle: Dry off, dress warmly if needed and let your breathing settle

you.title: You
you.noActivity: Finish a protocol to build your log.

sauna.title: Sauna
sauna.subtitle: Controlled heat, gradual cooling, full rest.
sauna.logExplanation: Adds +30 XP and keeps you on this protocol.
sauna.loggedMeta: +30 XP added. Keep resting before you leave.

guide.heat: The range is a ceiling, not a target. Leave earlier for dizziness, nausea, chest pain, confusion or distress.
guide.cool: Cool in air or with a comfortable shower. Cold water is optional; never force a plunge.
guide.rest: Sit until breathing, pulse and balance feel settled. Matching heat time is only a planning guide.
guide.repeat: Repeat only when fully settled. Drink to thirst, avoid alcohol and stop whenever symptoms return.

beginner.title: Beginner
beginner.subtitle: One or two short rounds, leave early

breath.practiceSubtitle: Follow a calm 4 second inhale and 6 second exhale.
breath.slowSubtitle: Breathe evenly and comfortably. Do not force the depth.
breath.resetSubtitle: Let the exhale run a little longer than the inhale. No breath holds.
breath.recoverySubtitle: Use a gentle longer exhale and stop if you feel lightheaded.
breath.completeSubtitle: Your full guided session is complete.
breath.leaveMessage: This guided session will not be completed.

diet.title: Nordic Diet
diet.subtitle: Plant-forward Nordic meal ideas
diet.disclaimer: Flexible meal ideas, not a personal prescription. Adjust portions, allergens and ingredients to your needs; use clinical guidance for medical diets.
diet.principlesMeta: What to favor and what to limit
diet.mealPlanMeta: Six fish meals, more plants, flexible portions

eat.fish: Fish and seafood several times weekly
eat.wholeGrains: Oats, rye, barley and whole-grain bread
eat.roots: Root vegetables, cabbage, potatoes and greens
eat.dairy: Plain lower-fat dairy or a suitable fortified alternative
eat.nuts: Nuts and seeds in small daily portions
eat.oil: Rapeseed or olive oil as default fat

avoid.highSaltSugar: Ready meals high in salt, added sugar or saturated fat
avoid.processedMeat: Processed meat as an everyday protein
avoid.refinedGrains: White bread and refined cereal as the base

day1.title: Coastal baseline
day1.breakfast: Oats with skyr, blueberries, walnuts and cinnamon.
day1.lunch: Barley and lentil bowl with beetroot, kale and carrots.
day1.dinner: Baked cod, potatoes, cabbage, peas and rapeseed-oil herb dressing.

day2.title: Fibre and pulses
day2.breakfast: Rye crispbread with egg, tomato and kefir.
day2.lunch: Pea soup with cabbage and whole-grain rye bread.
day2.dinner: White beans, barley, mushrooms, roasted roots and greens.

day3.title: Pulses and herring
day3.breakfast: Warm barley porridge with apple, lingonberries and pumpkin seeds.
day3.lunch: Chickpea, potato and cabbage soup with whole-grain bread.
day3.dinner: Herring or sardines, boiled potatoes, pickled onion and cucumber salad.

day4.title: Nordic workday
day4.breakfast: Skyr with oats, raspberries, flax and hazelnuts.
day4.lunch: Rye sandwich with egg or white beans, cucumber, lettuce and dill.
day4.dinner: Turkey or beans, barley, mushrooms, carrots and greens.

day5.title: Plant-forward
day5.breakfast: Oat pancakes with berries and plain yogurt.
day5.lunch: Pea soup with rye bread and a small hard-cheese serving.
day5.dinner: Roasted chickpeas, parsnip, beetroot and cabbage with plain yogurt or tahini.

day6.title: Active day
day6.breakfast: Oats, kefir, banana, blueberries and peanut or almond butter.
day6.lunch: Whole-grain wrap with lentils, spinach, carrots and yogurt-dill sauce.
day6.dinner: Salmon, potatoes, mushrooms and a large cabbage salad.

day7.title: Simple pantry day
day7.breakfast: Rye bread with cottage cheese, berries and seeds.
day7.lunch: Bean salad with potatoes, greens, apple and rapeseed oil.
day7.dinner: Mushroom and barley soup with carrots, leek and whole-grain bread.

day8.title: Market bowl
day8.breakfast: Barley porridge with pear, skyr and walnuts.
day8.lunch: Rye toast with white-bean spread, beet salad and cucumber.
day8.dinner: Lentil stew with root vegetables, kale and a spoon of yogurt.

day9.title: Mackerel day
day9.breakfast: Skyr, oats, blueberries, chia and almonds.
day9.lunch: Chickpeas on rye with tomato, herbs and cabbage slaw.
day9.dinner: Mackerel, boiled potatoes, green beans and fermented vegetables.

day10.title: Budget Nordic
day10.breakfast: Oatmeal with frozen berries, milk or kefir and seeds.
day10.lunch: Bean and barley soup with carrots, onion and whole-grain bread.
day10.dinner: Frozen cod or pollock, potatoes, peas and cabbage.

day11.title: Low effort
day11.breakfast: Rye crispbread with egg, skyr and an apple.
day11.lunch: Leftover beans over greens, potatoes and pickled cucumber.
day11.dinner: Sheet-pan chicken or chickpeas with roots, cabbage and yogurt sauce.

day12.title: Cultured foods, optional
day12.breakfast: Kefir bowl with oats, berries and flax.
day12.lunch: Rye sandwich with white beans, cucumber and optional fermented cabbage.
day12.dinner: Barley, mushrooms, peas, beetroot and greens with an optional plain-yogurt dressing.

day13.title: Cold season
day13.breakfast: Warm oats with apple, cinnamon, walnuts and skyr.
day13.lunch: Lentil and root vegetable stew with rye bread.
day13.dinner: Trout, cabbage, potatoes, carrots and broth.

day14.title: Repeatable Sunday
day14.lunch: White-bean rye toast with cucumber and dill.
day14.dinner: Root vegetable and bean stew with kale and whole-grain bread.

challenge.libraryMeta: This challenge is part of the library. It can return in the daily rotation, but it cannot be farmed for XP today.

forestHour.title: Forest hour
forestHour.subtitle: One hour outside, no rush
forestHour.detail: Walk at an easy pace for up to sixty minutes. Keep audio off and notice how your mood and energy feel before and after.

sunriseWalk.title: Morning daylight walk
sunriseWalk.subtitle: Outdoor light early in your day
sunriseWalk.detail: Go outside when practical during the first two hours after waking. Use normal eye protection when needed and never stare at the sun.

barefootSensoryWalk.title: Sensory walk
barefootSensoryWalk.subtitle: Ten careful minutes noticing the ground
barefootSensoryWalk.detail: Walk slowly and notice texture, temperature and balance. Bare feet are optional; keep shoes on with diabetes, reduced sensation or circulation, unsafe surfaces or uncomfortable temperatures.

coldWaterFace.title: Cool-water face splash
coldWaterFace.subtitle: Brief, optional and comfortable
coldWaterFace.detail: Splash comfortably cool water on your face once or twice. Keep breathing normally; this is a sensory practice, not a medical reset.

lakeDip.title: Open-water safety check
lakeDip.subtitle: Plan first; entering is not required
lakeDip.detail: Check water temperature, weather, currents, water quality, entry and exit, warm clothing and who would accompany you. Complete the check without entering the water.

fireEvening.title: Screen-free evening light
fireEvening.subtitle: LED candlelight and no scrolling
fireEvening.detail: Use an LED candle or dim lamp for thirty screen-free minutes. A real flame must stay supervised and follow current local fire rules.

snowWalk.title: Cold-weather walk
snowWalk.subtitle: Twenty weather-aware minutes
snowWalk.detail: Walk outside only when footing and weather are safe. Dress for the conditions and breathe in whatever comfortable way supports the pace.

phoneFreeTrail.title: Phone-free trail
phoneFreeTrail.subtitle: One hour offline
phoneFreeTrail.detail: Keep the phone silent in a bag and notice terrain, weather and breath. Retain emergency access when walking alone or in remote areas.

sitSpot.title: Sit spot
sitSpot.subtitle: Twenty minutes in one place
sitSpot.detail: Choose a legal, durable spot and sit still. Watch light, wind, birds, people and your breathing without forcing meditation.

rainWalk.title: Rain walk
rainWalk.subtitle: Dress properly, go anyway
rainWalk.detail: Walk for twenty minutes in rain or rough weather with proper clothing. Skip storms, ice hazards and unsafe wind.

shorelineObservation.title: Shoreline observation
shorelineObservation.subtitle: Thirty minutes by water
shorelineObservation.detail: Sit or walk near a lake, river, fjord or sea. Swimming is optional and belongs under cold-water safety rules.

leaveNoTrace.title: Leave no trace
leaveNoTrace.subtitle: Access paired with responsibility
leaveNoTrace.detail: With gloves, collect ordinary safe litter, stay off fragile ground and close gates. Never handle sharp, chemical, medical or unknown waste.

settings.themeMeta: Use system or set it manually

themeDescriptions.light: Bright polar surface for daytime use.

ro.introHint: Two drum hits. Dun dun. Then the crowd roars "ROO!". Wait for the countdown and keep speeding up.
ro.successMeta: You led the row. {{accuracy}}% in time. The stadium roars.
ro.successMetaPartial: {{accuracy}}% in time. Come back tomorrow, oarsman.

coach.losing: OFF RHYTHM. THE CROWD IS FADING

music.title: Listen to today's Norwegian single
music.story: A Norwegian single in today's protocol, opened through the user's own music service.
music.subtitle: Kygo, Flow Kingz & JMK feat. Lyng
music.instructions: Open the single in your music app, press play if needed, then come back here.
music.returnHint: Music can keep playing while you are here.
music.leaveFirst: Open a music app to begin
music.openErrorTitle: Could not open the music app
music.openErrorMessage: Check your connection and try the other service.
music.manualNote: Open the track in your music app, let it play, then return here when you are done.

q.cold: How well do you know the cold?
q.diet: How do you eat right now?

onboarding.roTitle: Feel it. You are the drummer.
onboarding.roHint: Two quick taps. The crowd roars back.
onboarding.roDone: That's the feeling. Now the rest.

reward.title: Keep the rhythm. Take 20% off.
reward.body: Land at least 5 of 7 scored rhythms to unlock 20% off your first year of Premium.
reward.ruleRetry: 1 second attempt if you need it
reward.terms: {{introductoryPrice}} for the first year, then {{standardPrice}}/year. Auto-renews.
reward.successBody: {{percentage}}% on beat. Your first-year discount is waiting on the paywall.
reward.retryBody: {{percentage}}% on beat. You have one more attempt to reach the reward.
reward.finalMissBody: {{percentage}}% on beat. Your protocol is still ready without the reward.
reward.ready: RO! reward: 20% off your first year

qHint.goals: Choose up to three. Your protocol will lean toward them.
qHint.cold: This sets a conservative starting point, not a test of toughness.
qHint.sauna: We adapt heat days to the access you actually have.
qHint.nature: A city park counts. We build around what is reachable.
qHint.diet: No guilt and no cleanse. Just tell us what is typical.

benefits.title: Four levers. One daily protocol.
benefits.body: Short actions across cold, heat, nature and food create a routine you can actually repeat.

sauna.title: Heat is only half the ritual.
sauna.body: Norwaymaxxing favors control over endurance. Sauna is optional, and more is not automatically better.

items.cool: Cool gradually and stop if you feel unwell
items.rest: Rest at least as long as you heated

evidence.title: Evidence before hype.
evidence.body: The app is a lifestyle habit system, not medical treatment. Exact doses stay conservative where evidence is limited.

items.safety: Conservative safety rules for heat and cold

nature.title: Friluftsliv starts at your door.
nature.body: You do not need a fjord. Frequent, ordinary time outside matters more than a perfect location.

items.nearby: Use the nature you can reach often
items.weather: Go out in ordinary weather, with suitable clothing
items.care: Respect local rules and leave nature as you found it

diet.title: Nordic food is a pattern, not a cleanse.
diet.body: Build most meals from plants, whole grains and flexible protein choices. No food needs a moral label.

items.grains: Whole grains, potatoes, nuts and seeds

community.title: You are joining the first wave.
community.body: A new community is building Nordic habits one day at a time. Start where you are and let your streak show the work.

ro.title: Now make it loud.
ro.body: RO! turns Norway's rowing chant into a quick rhythm game. Two taps start the wave, then you hold the beat.

result.title: This is why your plan can stick.
result.body: You do not need to plan five habits yourself. Norwaymaxxing turns your starting point into one short protocol for today, then adapts the next step as you build consistency.

items.clear: One clear protocol instead of five separate routines
items.adaptive: Cold, sauna and nature tasks match your access and experience
items.feedback: Norway Score shows what you completed, not what you intended

ready.title: Your 14-day Nordic protocol is ready.
ready.body: Day one is short on purpose. Start today, earn your first signal and let the plan build with your consistency.

benefit.nature: All nature challenges and the full RO! activity

paywall.cancelAnytime: Cancel anytime in the App Store
paywall.unavailable: Subscriptions are temporarily unavailable. Try again in a moment.
paywall.pending: Your purchase is still being processed by the App Store.
paywall.entitlementMissing: The purchase was received, but premium access is not active yet. Try Restore.
paywall.purchaseFailed: The purchase could not be completed. Please try again.
paywall.nothingToRestore: No active Norwaymaxxing subscription was found for this Apple ID.
paywall.restoreSuccess: Your premium access has been restored.
paywall.restoreFailed: Purchases could not be restored. Please try again.
